text.title: YAML Frontmatter Schema for SDD Documents
text.summary: Required fields, valid enum values, and quoting rules for SDD document frontmatter.

rule.statement: Every SDD document must open with a YAML frontmatter block bounded by triple-dash delimiters on their own lines, with no content before the opening delimiter.
rule.rationale: SSGs and parsers treat anything before the first `---` as body text. A missing or offset delimiter silently discards all metadata.

rule.statement: Always quote version numbers and IDs with special characters: `version: "1.0.0"`, `feature_id: "042-auth-refactor"`.
rule.rationale: Bare `1.0.0` parses as a float in YAML, producing `1.0`. Bare IDs with leading zeros or dashes may trigger unexpected coercions.

rule.statement: Use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD) for all date fields. Do not quote dates.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to locale-specific formats without explicit instruction. ISO dates sort lexicographically and parse unambiguously across all YAML parsers.

list.item: constitution: type, version, status, created, updated, author
roadmap: type, version, status, created, updated
spec: type, feature_id, version, status, priority, created, updated
design: type, feature_id, version, status, created, updated, spec_version
implementation-plan: type, feature_id, version, status, created, updated, design_version, total_tasks
task: type, task_id, feature_id, status, priority, created, complexity, estimated_tokens

rule.statement: status must be one of: draft, review, approved, active, superseded, archived.
rule.rationale: Consistent status values enable agent routing; `WIP`, `wip`, `in-review` observed in the wild all break enum checks.

rule.statement: priority must be one of: P0, P1, P2.
rule.rationale: P0 = blocker, P1 = critical, P2 = nice-to-have. More granular schemes are rarely honored in practice.

rule.statement: complexity must be one of: low, medium, high. Never use time estimates.
rule.rationale: Token and complexity estimates are reliable; time estimates are not. See project constitution.

example.code: --- type: spec feature_id: "026-knowledge-infrastructure" version: "1.0.0" status: draft priority: P1 created: 2026-01-20 updated: 2026-01-25 depends_on: [] ---
example.note: All required fields present; version quoted; dates unquoted ISO format.

example.code: --- type: spec feature_id: 026-knowledge-infrastructure version: 1.0.0 status: WIP created: 01-20-2026 ---
example.note: feature_id unquoted (leading digit), version parses as float, status not in enum, date not ISO.

text.reference: Copy-paste frontmatter blocks for all SDD document types
